---
title: "per_timepoint_fisher_aa — Olive report"
subtitle: "ribostall stall_sites — enrichment stats"
date: 2026-05-17
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    embed-resources: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
  pdf:
    toc: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{needspace}
tbl-colwidths: [26, 20, 17, 17, 20]
execute:
  echo: false
---

## Composite figure

![3x3 grid of BWM-vs-control amino-acid volcano panels: rows are timepoints (day 0 / day 5 / day 10) and columns are ribosome sites (A / P / E). Each panel plots -log10(FDR) against log2(odds ratio); triangles mark FDR<0.05 hits and circles are non-significant. Glyphs are coloured by amino-acid property (acidic = red, basic = blue, hydrophobic = green, neutral = orange, polar = purple). Day-0 panels are richly populated and span the widest log2(OR) range; day-5 and day-10 panels collapse toward the origin.](../../plots/per_timepoint_fisher/composite/aa_fisher_composite.png)

Each glyph is a single amino acid at one (timepoint, site) BH-FDR family of 20; horizontal dashed line is FDR = 0.05 and vertical dashed lines mark +/-0.5 log2(OR). Positive log2(OR) means BWM-enriched relative to control; negative means BWM-depleted.

---

## Overview

- **CSV:** `per_timepoint_fisher_aa.csv`
- **Family (from `_INDEX.md`):** `per_timepoint_fisher`
- **Sister CSV(s):** `per_timepoint_fisher_codon.csv`
- **Test:** Fisher's exact (two-sided), BWM vs control 2x2 contingency per (timepoint, site, aa); BH-FDR within each (timepoint, site) family of 20 AAs
- **Design:** Per (timepoint, site, aa) 2x2 BWM-vs-control contingency tested by two-sided Fisher's exact; replicates summed before the test (pseudoreplicated); BH-FDR applied independently within each of 9 (timepoint, site) families of 20 AAs (180 tests total).

### Family-level caveats

- **pseudorep** — 2 biological reps per (condition,timepoint) summed before Fisher's exact; the source script literally prints a pseudoreplication warning. Anti-conservative p-values.
- **large-N-Fisher-anticonservative** — pooled totals are in the thousands (e.g. BWM_total ~6091, control_total ~27732 at day_0); Fisher's exact returns vanishing p-values for tiny absolute deviations (odds_ratio 1.36 at p=1e-10). odds_ratio is the primary effect column, not p.
- **imbalanced-N** — BWM_total ~6091 vs control_total ~27732 at day_0 (~5x asymmetry); Fisher handles imbalance fine but downstream interpretation should not over-read odds ratios when one cell is small.
- **bh-per-(timepoint,site)** — BH-FDR is computed within each (timepoint,site) family of ~20 (aa) or ~61 (codon) tests. p_adj means "corrected within this slice", not across the whole CSV.

---

## Headline

180 tests (3 timepoints x 3 sites x 20 AAs), 27 hits at p_adj<0.05 (15.0%), 35 at p_adj<0.10 (19.4%). Per-(timepoint, site) cell n_sig at p_adj<0.05: day_0 A=7, day_0 P=5, day_0 E=6; day_5 A=1, P=1, E=5; day_10 A=1, P=0, E=1. Of 60 (site, aa) cells, 21 are concordant in OR direction across all 3 timepoints (9 enriched in BWM, 12 depleted in BWM); 39 show at least one sign change across the 3 timepoint values. Only 1 of those 39 has the sign change at p_adj<0.05 on both opposite-sign rows: E:K (day_0 log2OR=+0.480, p_adj=2.87e-15; day_5 log2OR=-0.195, p_adj=4.93e-3; day_10 log2OR=-0.035, ns). Largest-magnitude single-cell signals at p_adj<0.05 (mixing directions): day_0 A:N -0.755 (rare-aa: BWM_k=134), day_0 P:R +0.519, day_0 E:R +0.482, day_0 E:K +0.480 (file-min p_adj), day_0 E:Q -0.467, day_0 P:P -0.460, day_0 A:K +0.439, day_10 A:A -0.415, day_0 A:R +0.381. Largest-magnitude cells in the cross-timepoint concordant set (mean log2OR across the 3 timepoints, single sign throughout): P:K +0.253 (BWM-enriched, 2/3 cell-sig), P:R +0.239, A:R +0.218, A:T -0.178. Treat per-cell n_sig differences across timepoints as power-modulated by the BWM/control N imbalance (control:BWM ratio 4.55:1 at day_0, 0.94:1 at day_5, 1.27:1 at day_10), not as direction-of-effect biology.

## Top hits

The effect column is `log2_OR` (the log2 of the `odds_ratio` column). Direction is fixed by the BWM-vs-control contingency layout: positive log2(OR) = BWM-enriched, negative = BWM-depleted. `p_value` is the raw two-sided Fisher's exact p; `p_adj` is BH-corrected within each (timepoint, site) family of 20 AAs. Sub-tables below are split per-direction within each (timepoint, site) group. *Rows with `p_adj` < 0.10; up to 5 per direction, ranked by `|log2_OR|` descending.*

The two summary tables that follow the per-(timepoint, site) blocks (cross-timepoint direction concordance and direction-flip cells) are reproduced under "Cross-timepoint summary" at the end of this section.

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### day_0, A site — Enriched (BWM > control)

| amino acid | effect (`log2_OR`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| K (lysine) | +0.439 (OR=1.356) | 1.16e-10 | 2.31e-09 |  |
| R (arginine) | +0.381 (OR=1.302) | 3.50e-06 | 2.34e-05 |  |
| G (glycine) | +0.283 (OR=1.217) | 1.35e-04 | 6.72e-04 |  |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### day_0, A site — Depleted (BWM < control)

| amino acid | effect (`log2_OR`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| N (asparagine) | -0.755 (OR=0.593) | 3.17e-09 | 3.17e-08 |  |
| T (threonine) | -0.339 (OR=0.791) | 1.50e-03 | 5.98e-03 |  |
| F (phenylalanine) | -0.321 (OR=0.800) | 5.90e-03 | 1.98e-02 |  |
| D (aspartate) | -0.203 (OR=0.869) | 1.47e-02 | 4.21e-02 |  |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### day_0, P site — Enriched (BWM > control)

| amino acid | effect (`log2_OR`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| R (arginine) | +0.519 (OR=1.433) | 2.32e-10 | 4.65e-09 |  |
| K (lysine) | +0.360 (OR=1.283) | 1.05e-07 | 1.05e-06 |  |
| G (glycine) | +0.195 (OR=1.145) | 7.10e-03 | 2.96e-02 |  |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### day_0, P site — Depleted (BWM < control)

| amino acid | effect (`log2_OR`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| P (proline) | -0.460 (OR=0.727) | 1.95e-05 | 1.30e-04 |  |
| S (serine) | -0.281 (OR=0.823) | 7.40e-03 | 2.96e-02 |  |
| T (threonine) | -0.224 (OR=0.856) | 2.48e-02 | 7.07e-02 |  |
| E (glutamate) | -0.192 (OR=0.875) | 1.68e-02 | 5.59e-02 |  |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### day_0, E site — Enriched (BWM > control)

| amino acid | effect (`log2_OR`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| R (arginine) | +0.482 (OR=1.396) | 1.09e-08 | 1.09e-07 |  |
| K (lysine) | +0.480 (OR=1.395) | 1.43e-16 | 2.87e-15 | iid-amp |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### day_0, E site — Depleted (BWM < control)

| amino acid | effect (`log2_OR`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Q (glutamine) | -0.467 (OR=0.723) | 2.49e-05 | 1.66e-04 |  |
| S (serine) | -0.375 (OR=0.771) | 4.53e-04 | 2.26e-03 |  |
| Y (tyrosine) | -0.371 (OR=0.773) | 7.50e-03 | 3.00e-02 |  |
| T (threonine) | -0.248 (OR=0.842) | 1.43e-02 | 4.78e-02 |  |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### day_5, A site — Enriched (BWM > control)

| amino acid | effect (`log2_OR`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| L (leucine) | +0.280 (OR=1.215) | 2.49e-04 | 4.98e-03 |  |
| P (proline) | +0.236 (OR=1.177) | 1.84e-02 | 8.62e-02 |  |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### day_5, A site — Depleted (BWM < control)

| amino acid | effect (`log2_OR`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| N (asparagine) | -0.255 (OR=0.838) | 7.80e-03 | 5.17e-02 |  |
| E (glutamate) | -0.180 (OR=0.883) | 5.50e-03 | 5.17e-02 |  |
| I (isoleucine) | -0.177 (OR=0.884) | 2.16e-02 | 8.62e-02 |  |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### day_5, P site — Enriched (BWM > control)

| amino acid | effect (`log2_OR`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| K (lysine) | +0.211 (OR=1.157) | 2.20e-03 | 4.43e-02 |  |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### day_5, P site — Depleted (BWM < control)

| amino acid | effect (`log2_OR`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V (valine) | -0.196 (OR=0.873) | 8.40e-03 | 8.38e-02 |  |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### day_5, E site — Enriched (BWM > control)

| amino acid | effect (`log2_OR`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| P (proline) | +0.358 (OR=1.282) | 4.89e-04 | 4.93e-03 |  |
| A (alanine) | +0.299 (OR=1.230) | 1.40e-03 | 9.35e-03 |  |
| T (threonine) | +0.219 (OR=1.164) | 1.81e-02 | 6.05e-02 |  |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### day_5, E site — Depleted (BWM < control)

| amino acid | effect (`log2_OR`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| G (glycine) | -0.202 (OR=0.869) | 8.30e-03 | 3.32e-02 |  |
| K (lysine) | -0.195 (OR=0.874) | 4.93e-04 | 4.93e-03 |  |
| E (glutamate) | -0.174 (OR=0.886) | 7.20e-03 | 3.32e-02 |  |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### day_10, A site — Depleted (BWM < control)

| amino acid | effect (`log2_OR`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| A (alanine) | -0.415 (OR=0.750) | 1.45e-04 | 2.91e-03 |  |

### day_10, P site

*(no rows clear `p_adj` < 0.10; closest are P:I, P:K, P:L all at `p_adj` = 0.148, log2(OR) ~+0.16 to +0.21.)*

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### day_10, E site — Enriched (BWM > control)

| amino acid | effect (`log2_OR`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| R (arginine) | +0.388 (OR=1.308) | 9.29e-05 | 1.86e-03 |  |

### Cross-timepoint summary (two ranking tables)

#### Cross-timepoint direction concordance

21 of 60 (site, aa) cells have the same OR direction in all 3 timepoints. Sorted by |mean log2OR| across the 3 timepoint values. `#sig` is the count (out of 3) of timepoints where this row reaches p_adj<0.05; `min_p_adj` is the smallest of the 3 per-timepoint values.

| direction | cell (site:aa) | mean log2OR | per-tp log2OR (d0, d5, d10) | #sig (FDR<0.05) | min p_adj | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| enriched | P:K | +0.253 | +0.36, +0.21, +0.19 | 2/3 | 1.05e-06 | multi-tp-coherent |
| enriched | P:R | +0.239 | +0.52, +0.09, +0.10 | 1/3 | 4.65e-09 | single-tp-driven |
| enriched | A:R | +0.218 | +0.38, +0.13, +0.15 | 1/3 | 2.34e-05 | single-tp-driven |
| depleted | A:T | -0.178 | -0.34, -0.05, -0.14 | 1/3 | 5.98e-03 | single-tp-driven |
| enriched | A:W | +0.172 | +0.09, +0.12, +0.31 | 0/3 | 3.96e-01 | rare-aa |
| depleted | P:T | -0.166 | -0.22, -0.04, -0.23 | 0/3 | 7.07e-02 |  |
| enriched | A:H | +0.153 | +0.23, +0.11, +0.12 | 0/3 | 1.90e-01 |  |
| depleted | A:C | -0.146 | -0.08, -0.14, -0.22 | 0/3 | 4.95e-01 | rare-aa |
| depleted | P:A | -0.137 | -0.09, -0.13, -0.19 | 0/3 | 1.64e-01 |  |
| depleted | P:S | -0.136 | -0.28, -0.11, -0.02 | 1/3 | 2.96e-02 | single-tp-driven |
| depleted | P:C | -0.118 | -0.16, -0.12, -0.07 | 0/3 | 6.49e-01 | rare-aa |
| enriched | P:L | +0.117 | +0.04, +0.11, +0.20 | 0/3 | 1.48e-01 |  |
| depleted | P:E | -0.108 | -0.19, -0.12, -0.01 | 0/3 | 5.59e-02 |  |
| depleted | P:D | -0.091 | -0.10, -0.02, -0.15 | 0/3 | 1.64e-01 |  |
| depleted | E:D | -0.084 | -0.12, -0.05, -0.08 | 0/3 | 3.47e-01 |  |
| depleted | E:E | -0.080 | -0.05, -0.17, -0.02 | 1/3 | 3.32e-02 | single-tp-driven |
| enriched | E:L | +0.065 | +0.02, +0.15, +0.02 | 0/3 | 1.19e-01 |  |
| depleted | P:N | -0.049 | -0.09, -0.01, -0.05 | 0/3 | 5.72e-01 |  |
| depleted | P:F | -0.046 | -0.01, -0.06, -0.07 | 0/3 | 7.34e-01 |  |
| depleted | E:H | -0.045 | -0.04, -0.05, -0.04 | 0/3 | 7.43e-01 |  |
| depleted | E:N | -0.043 | -0.04, -0.05, -0.04 | 0/3 | 5.96e-01 |  |

#### Direction-flip cells across timepoints

39 of 60 (site, aa) cells show at least one sign change across the 3 timepoint values. Only 1 has the flip register at p_adj<0.05 on both opposite-sign rows. Sorted by max single-cell |log2OR|.

| cell (site:aa) | log2OR range | per-tp log2OR (sig at p_adj<0.05 marked `*`) | sig-flip | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| A:N | [-0.755, +0.302] | -0.75*, -0.25, +0.30 | no | rare-aa (d10 BWM_k=267) |
| E:W | [-0.211, +0.621] | -0.21, -0.16, +0.62 | no | rare-aa |
| E:C | [-0.310, +0.520] | -0.31, -0.21, +0.52 | no | rare-aa |
| E:R | [-0.095, +0.482] | +0.48*, -0.10, +0.39* | no |  |
| E:K | [-0.195, +0.480] | +0.48*, -0.19*, -0.04 | yes (d0 vs d5 both p_adj<0.05) | iid-amp (d0 cell only) |
| E:Q | [-0.467, +0.121] | -0.47*, -0.06, +0.12 | no |  |
| P:P | [-0.460, +0.025] | -0.46*, +0.03, -0.22 | no |  |
| A:K | [-0.062, +0.439] | +0.44*, -0.06, +0.13 | no |  |
| A:A | [-0.415, +0.031] | -0.08, +0.03, -0.42* | no |  |
| P:Q | [-0.255, +0.378] | -0.26, +0.20, +0.38 | no |  |
| E:S | [-0.375, +0.009] | -0.37*, +0.01, -0.02 | no |  |
| E:Y | [-0.371, +0.243] | -0.37*, +0.24, +0.21 | no |  |
| E:P | [-0.133, +0.358] | -0.15, +0.36*, -0.13 | no |  |
| A:Q | [-0.201, +0.336] | -0.20, +0.11, +0.34 | no |  |
| A:F | [-0.321, +0.116] | -0.32*, +0.12, -0.01 | no |  |

(15 highest-magnitude flip cells shown; 24 more lower-magnitude flip cells are in the data but all have max |log2OR| < 0.30.)

### Flag glossary

- `iid-amp` — single per-timepoint `p_adj` falls below 1e-10. The magnitude of `-log10(p_adj)` is inflated by Fisher's anti-conservative behaviour at large pooled N + correlated stall positions; rank by effect size, not by p magnitude.
- `rare-aa` — at least one of the 3 per-timepoint rows for this cell has `BWM_k` < 100 (or similarly low `ctrl_k`). Odds-ratio estimate is unstable; large |log2OR| values may reflect sampling noise rather than biology.
- `multi-tp-coherent` — cell reaches `p_adj` < 0.05 in 2 or 3 of 3 timepoints; signal is reproducible across the time axis.
- `single-tp-driven` — cell reaches `p_adj` < 0.05 in exactly 1 of 3 timepoints; the cross-timepoint mean is carried by that one timepoint and is exploratory across the others.

## Numbers at a glance
- `n_tests`: 180 (3 timepoints x 3 sites x 20 AAs)
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.05): 27 (15.0%)
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.10): 35 (19.4%)
- `min adjusted-p`: 2.8687614397053295e-15 (day_0 E K, log2OR=+0.480, BWM_k=983/n=6091, ctrl_k=3362/n=27732)
- `p_floor`: n/a — Fisher's exact has no analytic floor; effective discreteness floor at small (k_bwm + k_ctrl) is captured by `small-bh-family-discreteness`
- Cells with p_adj < 1e-10: 1 of 180 (only day_0 E K); flagged `iid-amp`
- Cross-(timepoint, site) BH families and their n_sig at p_adj<0.05:

| timepoint | A | P | E | BWM_total | control_total | ctrl:BWM ratio |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| day_0 | 7/20 (min p 2.31e-09) | 5/20 (min p 4.65e-09) | 6/20 (min p 2.87e-15) | 6091 | 27732 | 4.55 |
| day_5 | 1/20 (min p 4.98e-03) | 1/20 (min p 4.43e-02) | 5/20 (min p 4.93e-03) | 11935 | 11177 | 0.94 |
| day_10 | 1/20 (min p 2.91e-03) | 0/20 (min p 1.48e-01) | 1/20 (min p 1.86e-03) | 6945 | 8788 | 1.27 |

- Cross-timepoint (site, aa) cells, all 3 timepoints same OR direction: 21/60 (9 BWM-enriched, 12 BWM-depleted)
- Cross-timepoint (site, aa) cells with at least one sign change: 39/60
- Cells with sign change at p_adj<0.05 on both opposite-sign rows: 1 (E:K)

## Biological interpretation

**Day-0 BWM ribosomes are enriched for basic residues (K, R) and depleted for asparagine at A and P sites (concrete).** Both Lys and Arg show large, FDR-confirmed BWM-enrichment at all three sites at day 0 — E:K (+0.48, p_adj=2.9e-15), E:R (+0.48), P:R (+0.52), P:K (+0.36), A:K (+0.44), A:R (+0.38). Co-enrichment of K and R at the A site is consistent with electrostatic slowing in the exit tunnel: positively charged nascent-chain residues interact with the negatively charged ribosomal RNA backbone and slow elongation through the A/P/E corridor. The mirror image at day 0 A site is N (-0.76 at p_adj=3.2e-8) — depletion of a small polar residue is harder to anchor mechanistically and may reflect compositional displacement by the over-represented basics rather than an N-specific stalling effect.

**The day-0 P-site proline depletion is mechanistically anchored (concrete).** P-site P at day 0 is depleted at log2(OR) = -0.46 (p_adj=1.3e-4). Pro is the canonical exit-tunnel stalling residue (eIF5A/EF-P-dependent rescue): if BWM has *fewer* stalls with P at the P site than control, the BWM stall population is shifted away from poly-Pro stall events at day 0 specifically. This is one of the few day-0 hits whose direction (depletion in BWM) cuts against a "BWM has more conventional stall events" reading, and is worth flagging at the synthesis layer.

**The K-at-E flip between day 0 and day 5 is the file's only FDR-significant direction change (exploratory).** E:K is BWM-enriched at day 0 (+0.48, p_adj=2.9e-15, the file minimum) and BWM-depleted at day 5 (-0.20, p_adj=4.9e-3). Both rows clear FDR<0.05 in opposite directions, and the d0 row is flagged `iid-amp` — meaning the p magnitude is amplified by large-N anti-conservatism (BWM:control ratio 4.55:1 at day 0). Treating this as a real biphasic Lys-at-E phenomenon would require corroboration in the codon-resolution sister CSV (does AAG-at-E or AAA-at-E flip in the same direction?) and in the within-condition Fisher contrasts (is the d0->d5 step the carrier, or is the per-timepoint design picking up a small-effect crossing zero?). Hold as a hypothesis, not a finding.

**The strong day-0 dominance is largely a power story, not a biology story (concrete).** Per-(timepoint, site) hit counts collapse from 18 at day 0 to 7 at day 5 to 2 at day 10, while file-minimum `p_adj` spans 12 orders of magnitude over the same gradient (2.9e-15 -> 4.9e-3 -> 1.9e-3). The control:BWM N ratio is 4.55:1 at day 0, 0.94:1 at day 5, 1.27:1 at day 10 — moderate effect sizes at day 0 inflate to extreme p magnitudes that the same effect sizes at later timepoints would not reach. The `n-asymmetry-affects-power-not-direction` caveat applies: per-cell hit-count differences are power-modulated, not evidence that BWM-control divergence is concentrated at day 0 in biology.

**A subset of cross-timepoint concordant cells points at stable BWM-vs-control composition differences (exploratory).** P:K (+0.25 mean, sig in 2/3 timepoints), P:R (+0.24), A:R (+0.22), and A:T (-0.18) are direction-consistent across all three timepoints with moderate magnitudes. The basic-residue enrichment direction matches the day-0 headline but holds across the time axis at smaller magnitudes — consistent with a stable BWM-vs-control compositional offset on top of which the day-0 amplification rides. These cells are worth carrying into the codon sister CSV (do they aggregate from AGA/CGC/CGT at codon resolution?) and the within-condition binomial (are they baseline features shared by both conditions, or BWM-specific?).

**What to carry forward.** *Concrete*: the day-0 basic-residue enrichment (K/R at A/P/E) and P-site Pro depletion; the day_10 P-site has no statistically significant differences at FDR<0.05 and the day_10 A and E sites each carry only one FDR<0.05 hit. *Exploratory*: the E:K direction flip (needs codon-level and within-condition corroboration); the 21-cell cross-timepoint concordant set as a stable BWM-control offset hypothesis. *Noise*: the day-10 P-site row is structurally flat (min p_adj = 0.148) and contributes nothing; the file's largest single-cell magnitudes that are `rare-aa` flagged (A:N -0.755 sits at BWM_k=134 — high enough to count, but adjacent rows in the same cell with smaller k are unstable).

## Methods
Fisher's exact (two-sided) on a 2x2 contingency BWM_count vs control_count for each (timepoint, site, aa), with BH-FDR correction within each (timepoint, site) family of 20 AAs. Effect column is `odds_ratio` (re-expressed as log2OR in this report's tables for symmetry with sister files); test statistic and exact p are returned by `scipy.stats.fisher_exact`. The p-correction column is `p_adj` (BH per (timepoint, site) family). The test answers "is the BWM-vs-control AA composition at this site different at this timepoint?". It does *not* answer "is each condition's per-codon enrichment vs background informative" (that is the `within_condition_binomial` family) and *not* "is the BWM-vs-control difference itself shifting across timepoints" (that requires a 3-way model or an explicit between-timepoint contrast — see the `between_timepoint_wilcoxon` family for the within-condition variant of that question).

## What the caveats mean for reading this file

- **pseudorep.** 2x2 contingencies pool replicates by design; per-replicate variation is invisible to the statistic, so p-values are anti-conservative — read `odds_ratio` as the primary effect column and treat the smallest p-magnitudes as inflated, not as evidence of additional certainty.
- **large-N-Fisher-anticonservative.** With many thousands of pooled stall events and stalls correlated on shared motifs/transcripts, Fisher's effective N is smaller than `k_total`; treat tiny p magnitudes (`p_adj` < 1e-10) as inflated and rank by `log2_OR`.
- **imbalanced-N.** At day 0, control_total (27732) dwarfs BWM_total (6091); Fisher concentrates its information in the larger arm and inflates `p_adj` at moderate effects — read day-0 magnitudes against day-5/day-10 with the imbalance in mind, not as a count of "more biology."
- **bh-per-(timepoint,site).** BH is run independently within each of the 9 (timepoint, site) families of 20; two cells with the same raw p in different cells can land at different `p_adj` — rank within a (timepoint, site) cell, not across the full 180-test grid.
- **small-bh-family-discreteness.** At family size 20 BH adjacency steps are coarse and `p_adj` values cluster at a small number of distinct levels within a single cell (day_5,A has 5 rows tied at `p_adj` = 0.0517 and below; day_10,P top of family at `p_adj` = 0.148) — treat the 0.05 threshold as a binary flag, not a precise rank.
- **rare-aa-low-count.** Rare AAs (W, M, C and several others by cell) have `BWM_k` or `ctrl_k` under 100 in some cells; OR estimates and CIs are unstable at small k. Flagged rows are reported but the magnitude weight should account for the count.
- **OR-direction-anchor.** OR > 1 = BWM-enriched, OR < 1 = BWM-depleted at this (timepoint, site, aa); stated here so downstream consumers do not invert the direction.
- **n-asymmetry-affects-power-not-direction.** The 12-OOM gap between min `p_adj` at day 0 (2.87e-15) and day 5 (4.93e-3) or day 10 (1.86e-3) is consistent with day 0's 4.55:1 control:BWM ratio amplifying moderate effects; per-cell hit counts are power-modulated, not direction-of-effect biology.

---

## Plots — individual

![Day-0 A-site BWM-vs-control volcano: log2(OR) on x, -log10(FDR) on y; triangles are FDR<0.05. Lysine (K, basic blue) at +0.44 log2(OR) and asparagine (N, polar purple) at -0.76 are the largest-magnitude FDR<0.05 hits; arginine (R, basic blue) and glycine (G, neutral orange) are also enriched; threonine (T, polar), phenylalanine (F, hydrophobic green), and aspartate (D, acidic red) are modestly depleted.](../../plots/per_timepoint_fisher/individual/day_0_A_volcano.png)

![Day-0 P-site BWM-vs-control volcano: arginine (R, basic blue) at +0.52 log2(OR) and lysine (K, basic) at +0.36 are the top-enriched FDR<0.05 hits; proline (P, neutral orange) at -0.46 is the strongest depletion; glycine (G, neutral) and serine (S, polar) are marginally significant.](../../plots/per_timepoint_fisher/individual/day_0_P_volcano.png)

![Day-0 E-site BWM-vs-control volcano: lysine (K, basic blue) at +0.48 log2(OR), p_adj=2.9e-15 sits highest on the y-axis (file-minimum); arginine (R, basic) is also enriched; glutamine (Q, polar), serine (S, polar), tyrosine (Y, hydrophobic green), and threonine (T, polar) are FDR<0.05 depletions.](../../plots/per_timepoint_fisher/individual/day_0_E_volcano.png)

![Day-5 A-site BWM-vs-control volcano: nearly all points collapse to the origin; leucine (L, hydrophobic green) at +0.28 log2(OR) is the only FDR<0.05 hit. Scale and dashed FDR/effect-size guides are identical to the day-0 panels for cross-timepoint comparability.](../../plots/per_timepoint_fisher/individual/day_5_A_volcano.png)

![Day-5 P-site BWM-vs-control volcano: a flat panel; lysine (K, basic blue) at +0.21 log2(OR) is the single FDR<0.05 hit, sitting just above the FDR threshold line.](../../plots/per_timepoint_fisher/individual/day_5_P_volcano.png)

![Day-5 E-site BWM-vs-control volcano: 5 FDR<0.05 hits clustered tightly around the origin; proline (P, neutral orange) and alanine (A, hydrophobic green) are enriched (+0.30 to +0.36 log2(OR)); lysine (K, basic blue), glycine (G, neutral), and glutamate (E, acidic red) are depleted (-0.17 to -0.20).](../../plots/per_timepoint_fisher/individual/day_5_E_volcano.png)

![Day-10 A-site BWM-vs-control volcano: alanine (A, hydrophobic green) at -0.42 log2(OR) is the single FDR<0.05 hit; all other amino acids collapse below the FDR threshold line within the +/-0.3 log2(OR) band.](../../plots/per_timepoint_fisher/individual/day_10_A_volcano.png)

![Day-10 P-site BWM-vs-control volcano: no FDR<0.05 hits; all points fall below the FDR threshold and within the +/-0.4 log2(OR) band — the structurally flattest panel in the file (min `p_adj` = 0.148).](../../plots/per_timepoint_fisher/individual/day_10_P_volcano.png)

![Day-10 E-site BWM-vs-control volcano: arginine (R, basic blue) at +0.39 log2(OR) is the single FDR<0.05 hit; all other amino acids fall below the FDR threshold.](../../plots/per_timepoint_fisher/individual/day_10_E_volcano.png)
